City Council
Regular Meeting
12/15/2025 5:30 PM
Sullivan Chamber
Cambridge City Hall   795 Massachusetts Ave   Cambridge, MA 02139
Downloads:
Agenda Summary Agenda Packet
Final Actions
Call to Order
Roll Call
Pledge of Allegiance
Public Comment
I. City Manager's Agenda
1. CMA 2025 #291 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of four (4) members to the Cambridge Commission for Persons with Disabilities (CCPD). APPROVED [Unanimous]

AND CMA PLACED ON FILE

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Memo re 12.25 CCPD Board Nominations 1
b. Cecelia Cobb CCPD Recommendation 1a
c. Asherah Davis CCPD Recommendation 1b
d. Nieta Greene CCPD Recommendation 1c
e. Erum Sattar CCPD Recommendation 1e
2. CMA 2025 #292 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of the following people as members of the BEUDO Review Board. APPROVED [Unanimous]

AND CMA PLACED ON FILE

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Margery Davies BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2a
b. Kay Mammo BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2b
c. Peter Glick BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2c
d. Andrew Richardson BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2d
e. Liz Galloway BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2e
f. Joe Higgins BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2f
g. Vivan Malkani BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2g
h. Liz Reichart BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2h
i. Ben Silverman BEUDO Review Board Appointment 2i
3. CMA 2025 #293 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of Sue Walsh as a member of the Community Benefits Advisory Committee. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
4. CMA 2025 #294 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-66, regarding a dedicated position or function focused on triaging and directing senior residents to appropriate services and supports. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
5. CMA 2025 #295 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-64, regarding poison ivy control at Fresh Pond. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Response to CC POR 2025 - 148
6. CMA 2025 #296 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-57, regarding body-worn cameras. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. AR 25-57 CPD AR Reponse 12.15.25
7. CMA 2025 #297 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-54, regarding review of current crisis prevention protocols and deployment of mental health professionals. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. AR 25-54 CPD AR Reponse 12.15.25
8. CMA 2025 #298 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-49, regarding development of a policy for future private development. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. 20251211_PO_2025_118_CommunityBenefits_Response
9. CMA 2025 #299 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-40, regarding updates to the Community Benefits Ordinance. REFERRED [Unanimous]

TO THE ORDINANCE COMMITTEE AND CMA PLACED ON FILE

a. 25-40 - Response re Community Benefits Advisory Committee - final 12.15.25
b. AR 25-40 - Communication to CBAC
c. AR 25-40 - Amendments to Community Benefits Ordinance

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout

13. CMA 2025 #303 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, transmitting an update on federal grant funding. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. 251215 Federal Funding Memo (final draft UPDATED)
b. 251215 Federal Funding Update - Appendix (final draft UPDATED)
14. CMA 2025 #304 : Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $935,497.00 to the Grant Fund Human Service Programs Salary and Wages account ($67,493.00), to the Grant Fund Human Service Programs Other Ordinary Maintenance account ($858,004.00) and to the Grant Fund Human Service Programs Travel and Training account ($10,000.00). ORDER ADOPTED [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
II. Policy Order and Resolution List
1. POR 2025 #170 : Cancellation of the December 29, 2025 City Council Meeting. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Policy Order Printout
2. POR 2025 #171 : That the City Manager is requested to instruct the Community Development Department to reduce the recommendations for the Massachusetts Avenue subdistrict (MAS-12) from a maximum of 12 stories to 11 stories with ground floor active use/retail as consistent with the recommendation of the Mass Ave Planning Study. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIDDIQUI IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 15, 2025 Charter Right
Policy Order Printout
III. Calendar
Charter Right
1. POR 2025 #165 : That the City Manager is requested to instruct the Community Development Department to reduce the recommendations for the Inman Square zoning sub-district (CAM-10) from a maximum of 10 stories to 8 stories with ground floor active use/retail as consistent with the majority of the Cambridge Street corridor. Order Adopted [7 to 1]
Policy Order Printout
On the Table
2. CMA 2025 #113 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-22, regarding a request to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to open the publicly owned parking at the King Open/Cambridge Street Upper School Complex for either residential free parking or commercial parking opportunities during “off” hours. PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MAY 5, 2025 NO ACTION TAKEN
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
3. POR 2025 #76 : That the City Manager is requested to explore with the Government Operations Committee whether the functions of the Peace Commission may be improved and enhanced by bringing them within another City Commission or Department, such as the Human Rights Commission, and report back in a timely manner. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL MAY 19, 2025 PLACED ON THE TABLE JUNE 2, 2025 REMOVED FROM THE TABLE [Unanimous]
Policy Order Printout
4. RES 2025 #148 : That the City Council go on record thanking SEIU 32BJ for their vigilance, partnership and advocacy on behalf of unionized workers. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL JUNE 16, 2025 PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL JUNE 23, 2025 REMOVED FROM THE TABLE [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
Unfinished Business
5. ORD 2025 # 17 : An Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, relative to amend the Zoning Map and Articles 3.000, 17.000, and 19.000 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance as follows with the intent of establishing four new zoning districts for the Cambridge Street corridor PASSED TO A SECOND READING AS AMENDED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 8, 2025 ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED DECEMBER 22, 2025 TABLED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025 NO ACTION TAKEN
b. 20251204_zngamend_cambridge street petition_REVISED_CLEAN
c. 251208.Cambridge Street Zoning Ordinance 2025-17 - First Publication (full text)
6. ORD 2025 # 16 : An Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, relative to amend the Zoning Map and Articles 2.000, 3.000, 4.000, 5.000, 6.000, 11.000 17.000, and 20.000 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance as follows with the intent of establishing four new zoning districts for the Massachusetts Avenue corridor. NO ACTION TAKEN
c. 251208.Massachusetts Avenue Zoning Ordinance 2025-16 - First Publication (full text)
IV. Applications and Petitions
1. APP 2025 #43 : An application was received from Brian Chipman representing Curie.Bio, requesting permission for two (2) projecting blade signs at the premises numbered 196 Broadway approval has been received from Inspectional Services, Department of Public Works, Community Development Department and abutters proof of mailing has been provided. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Application and Petitions Printout
a. scanned application

VI. Resolutions
1. RES 2025 #275 : Congratulations to Patrol Officer Keith Albert on his retirement from the Cambridge Police Department. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
2. RES 2025 #276 : Congratulations to Sikha Sen on retiring from the Department of Human Services. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
3. RES 2025 #277 : Congratulations to Umme Banu on her retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
4. RES 2025 #278 : Congratulations to Lieutenant David Schofield on his retirement from the Cambridge Police Department. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
5. RES 2025 #279 : Congratulations to Charles Antinoro on his retirement from the Cambridge Housing Authority. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
6. RES 2025 #280 : Congratulations to Maria Flores on her retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
7. RES 2025 #281 : Congratulations to Lisa Munson on her retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
8. RES 2025 #282 : Congratulations to Lynette O'Brien on her retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
9. RES 2025 #283 : Congratulations to Thomas Sullivan on his retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
10. RES 2025 #284 : Congratulations to Maria Eby on her retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
11. RES 2025 #285 : Congratulations to Ronald Venancio on his retirement from the Cambridge Health Alliance. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
12. RES 2025 #286 : Congratulations to Vengerflutta Smith Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
13. RES 2025 #287 : Condolences on the death of Roger O'Sullivan Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
VII. Communications and Reports from Other City Officers
1. COF 2025 #159 : A communication was received from Paula Crane, Interim City Clerk, transmitting an update regarding legislative activity. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
Communication and Report from Other City Officers Printout
a. Updated Hearing Schedule 12.11.2025
b. PENDING HOME RULE PETITIONS DECEMBER 15, 2025
c. 2024-2025 Committee Referral List
d. Pending Zoning Petitions and Municipal Ordinances Chart
Appended Information
Awaiting Report Status Update
ARS-25-32 : Awaiting Report Status Update 12.15.25
a. Awaiting Report Status Update 12.15.25
Awaiting Report List
2. AR-25-29 : That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents.
3. AR-25-40 : The City Manager be and hereby is requested to direct the City Solicitor, in consultation with relevant City departments and the Community Benefits Advisory Committee, to draft amendments to the Community Benefits Ordinance that allow for the use of funds for capital expenses, provided that such expenses clearly advance the goals of the Community Benefits program. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
4. AR-25-48 : The City Manager is requested to consult with the appropriate City departments, including the City Solicitor’s Office, to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City’s role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations.
5. AR-25-49 : the City Manager is requested to work with the Community Development Department, the City Assessor, and all other relevant departments to develop a comprehensive policy for future private development projects in the city. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
6. AR-25-51 : The City Manager is requested to work with all relevant departments to seek input from the community as well as direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators and develop additional recommendations based on the discussion in the Ordinance Committee.
7. AR-25-54 : The City Manager is requested to work with the Cambridge Police Department to review current crisis prevention protocols, strengthen them by clearly defining the role and deployment of mental health professionals. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
8. AR-25-57 : The City Manager is requested to work with the Cambridge Police Department, the Law Department, and relevant stakeholders to develop and present to the City Council a proposed policy for the timely release of body-worn camera footage. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
9. AR-25-61 : The City Manager is requested to work with relevant departments, state agencies, and the property owner IQHQ to provide an update on efforts to maintain cleanliness and safety in the area on Alewife Paths near Russell Field.
10. AR-25-64 : That the City Manager is requested to work with relevant departments to report back to the City Council on current efforts towards poison ivy control in the Fresh Pond Master Plan as well as recommended improvements for signage and regular maintenance planning. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
11. AR-25-65 : That the City Manager is requested to confer with the Cambridge Department of Transportation and other relevant departments to examine how to improve connectivity north of Rindge Avenue during the Linear Park Reconstruction Project so that vulnerable road users can bypass Rindge Avenue and Cedar Street where possible. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
12. AR-25-66 : The City Manager is requested to confer with the Executive Director of the Council on Aging and other relevant City departments to explore the feasibility of creating a dedicated position or function focused on triaging and directing senior residents to appropriate services and supports. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
13. AR-25-67 : Assess opportunities to improve the consistency of how meeting information is posted and accessed on the City’s website – including clearer navigation paths, standardized posting practices, and improved user-friendliness – and report back to the City Council on recommended steps.
14. AR-25-68 : Streamline the permitting process, including but not limited to creating a central role dedicated to the permitting process, the creation of a unified online permitting portal, and standardized timelines, and report back to the City Council not later than March 2026.
15. AR-25-69 : Review the previous home rule petition and prepare a new petition that would allow Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee to be sent to the state legislature.
Adjournment


  Loading - Please Wait...

Shortcut Keys:

Alt + S - Save

Ctrl + S - Save (may not be compatible in all browsers)

Alt + C - Save and Close

Ctrl + Enter - Save and Close

Escape - Closes the form (same as Cancel)

Alt + Up Arrow - Previous Item (Saves if changed)

Alt + Down Arrow - Next Item (Saves if changed)
